title: Deep maroon sharara for the baraat
concept_summary: A stately baraat concept with a long velvet kameez over silk sharara trousers that flare from the waist, framed by a double dupatta with one drape over the head and one across the arms.

garment_breakdown.overall_form: A long kameez over sharara trousers, layered with two dupattas.
garment_breakdown.silhouette: A covered kameez over trousers that open into a full flare from the waist.
garment_breakdown.drape_or_layering: One dupatta is set over the head and a second is carried across the arms.
garment_breakdown.key_proportions: The long kameez balances the full sweep of the sharara trousers.

colour_story.palette_summary: A rich maroon deepened by burnished gold.
colour_story.placement: Maroon dominates the kameez and trousers with gold framing the yoke and borders.
colour_story.rationale: Maroon and gold read as warm, formal winter baraat colours.

fabrics_and_texture.finish_and_movement: A dense, matte pile with a substantial, structured fall.
A fluid silk that carries the trouser flare and the dupatta drape.

embellishment_plan.density: A balanced concentration of metalwork on the yoke and borders.
embellishment_plan.restraint_notes: Solid maroon ground is preserved between the worked zones.

coverage_and_drape.neckline: A covered V-neckline set into a dense embroidered yoke.
coverage_and_drape.back_and_midriff: The long kameez fully covers the torso, back and midriff.
coverage_and_drape.head_covering: One dupatta is draped over the head throughout.
coverage_and_drape.dupatta_or_saree_drape: A double dupatta: one over the head and one across the arms.

cultural_context.regional_direction: A broad Pakistani bridal influence guides the layered, covered formality.
interpretation_notes: Treated as one broad direction, not a rule for any community.
safeguards: The sharara is kept distinct from a gharara, flaring from the waist.

styling_notes: Pair with statement gold jewellery for the baraat.
construction_caveats: This is a concept visualisation only and is not a sewing pattern.
It does not guarantee that the garment can be constructed exactly as shown.
image_alt_text: A model in a maroon sharara set with a long kameez and a double dupatta for the baraat.
